perturbation.original_text: We will treat confidentially any information (whether written or oral) that either the Company or its financial advisor, J.P. Morgan & Co. Incorporated ("JPM"), or the Company's other representatives furnish to us in connection with a Possible Transaction involving the Company, together with analyses, compilations, studies or other documents prepared by us, or by our representatives (as defined below) which contain or otherwise reflect such information or our review of, or interest in, the Company (collectively, the "Evaluation Materials"). We recognize and acknowledge the competitive value of the Evaluation Materials and the damage that could result to the Company if the Evaluation Materials were used or disclosed except as authorized by this Agreement. The term "Evaluation Materials" includes information furnished to us orally or in writing (whatever the form or storage medium) or gathered by inspection, and regardless of whether such information is specifically identified as "confidential". The term "Evaluation Materials" does not include information which (i) is or becomes generally available to the public other than as a result of a disclosure by us or our representatives, (ii) was or becomes available to us on a non-confidential basis from a source other than the Company or its representatives, provided that such source is not prohibited from disclosing such information to us by a contractual, legal or fiduciary obligation to the Company or its representatives, or (iii) is independently developed by us.
perturbation.changed_text: We will treat confidentially any information (whether written or oral) that either the Company or its financial advisor, J.P. Morgan & Co. Incorporated ("JPM"), or the Company's other representatives furnish to us in connection with a Possible Transaction involving the Company, together with analyses, compilations, studies or other documents prepared by us, or by our representatives (as defined below) which contain or otherwise reflect such information or our review of, or interest in, the Company (collectively, the "Evaluation Materials"). We recognize and acknowledge the competitive value of the Evaluation Materials and the damage that could result to the Company if the Evaluation Materials were used or disclosed except as authorized by this Agreement. The term "Evaluation Materials" includes information furnished to us orally or in writing (whatever the form or storage medium) or gathered by inspection, and regardless of whether such information is specifically identified as "confidential", unless otherwise noted. The term "Evaluation Materials" does not include information which (i) is or becomes generally available to the public other than as a result of a disclosure by us or our representatives, (ii) was or becomes available to us on a non-confidential basis from a source other than the Company or its representatives, provided that such source is not prohibited from disclosing such information to us by a contractual, legal or fiduciary obligation to the Company or its representatives, or (iii) is independently developed by us.
perturbation.explanation: By adding "unless otherwise noted" to the definition of Evaluation Materials, it now contradicts the earlier part of the section that states "We will treat confidentially any information". This addition creates an ambiguity in whether all information is indeed to be treated confidentially, as it could be interpreted that some Evaluation Materials may not be subject to confidentiality depending on unspecified notes, thus creating in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: We will treat confidentially any information (whether written or oral) that either the Company or its financial advisor, J.P. Morgan & Co. Incorporated ("JPM"), or the Company's other representatives furnish to us in connection with a Possible Transaction involving the Company, together with analyses, compilations, studies or other documents prepared by us, or by our representatives (as defined below) which contain or otherwise reflect such information or our review of, or interest in, the Company (collectively, the "Evaluation Materials"). We recognize and acknowledge the competitive value of the Evaluation Materials and the damage that could result to the Company if the Evaluation Materials were used or disclosed except as authorized by this Agreement. The term "Evaluation Materials" includes information furnished to us orally or in writing (whatever the form or storage medium) or gathered by inspection, and regardless of whether such information is specifically identified as "confidential", unless otherwise noted. The term "Evaluation Materials" does not include information which (i) is or becomes generally available to the public other than as a result of a disclosure by us or our representatives, (ii) was or becomes available to us on a non-confidential basis from a source other than the Company or its representatives, provided that such source is not prohibited from disclosing such information to us by a contractual, legal or fiduciary obligation to the Company or its representatives, or (iii) is independently developed by us.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that 'We will treat confidentially any information...' and then defines 'Evaluation Materials' in a way that includes all information furnished. The changed text modifies the definition of 'Evaluation Materials' to include the phrase 'unless otherwise noted'. This creates a direct contradiction, as it implies that some Evaluation Materials may not be treated confidentially, conflicting with the initial statement of confidentiality. The contradicted text is essentially the same section as the changed text, so the contradiction is within the section itself and therefore a strong, in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: We and our representatives will keep the Evaluation Materials completely confidential; provided, however, that (i) any of such information may be disclosed to those of our directors, officers, employees, agents, representatives (including attorneys, accountants and financial advisors), lenders and other sources of financing (collectively, "our representatives") who we reasonably determine need to know such information for the purpose of evaluating a Possible Transaction between us and the Company (it being understood that our representatives shall be informed by us of the confidential nature of such information and shall be directed by us, and shall each agree to treat such information confidentially) and (ii) any other disclosure of such information may only be made if the Company consents in writing prior to any such disclosure.
perturbation.changed_text: We and our representatives will keep the Evaluation Materials confidential; provided, however, that (i) any of such information may be disclosed to those of our directors, officers, employees, agents, representatives (including attorneys, accountants and financial advisors), lenders and other sources of financing (collectively, "our representatives") who we determine need to know such information for any purpose (it being understood that our representatives shall not be informed by us of the confidential nature of such information and shall not be directed by us to treat such information confidentially) and (ii) any other disclosure of such information may only be made if the Company consents in writing prior to any such disclosure.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause emphasizes that representatives will be informed of the confidential nature of the Evaluation Materials. The changed clause removes the 'completely' from 'completely confidential', changes the purpose of knowing such information from "evaluating a Possible Transaction" to "any purpose", and eliminates the requirement to inform representatives of the confidential nature. This undermines the confidentiality commitment, creating an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: We and our representatives will keep the Evaluation Materials confidential; provided, however, that (i) any of such information may be disclosed to those of our directors, officers, employees, agents, representatives (including attorneys, accountants and financial advisors), lenders and other sources of financing (collectively, "our representatives") who we determine need to know such information for any purpose (it being understood that our representatives shall not be informed by us of the confidential nature of such information and shall not be directed by us to treat such information confidentially) and (ii) any other disclosure of such information may only be made if the Company consents in writing prior to any such disclosure. Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, in the event that a Possible Transaction is not consummated neither we nor our representatives shall use any of the Evaluation Materials for any purpose.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the original clause such that the representatives are no longer required to be informed about the confidential nature of the Evaluation Materials nor directed to treat them confidentially, changing the purpose of knowing such information from 'evaluating a Possible Transaction' to 'any purpose'. This contradicts the initial confidentiality commitment. The change creates a conflict because the contradicted text includes this change as well which directly goes against the initial commitment of confidentiality, creating a structural flaw/in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: In the event that we or any of our representatives receive a request or are required (by deposition, interrogatory, request for documents, subpoena, civil investigative demand or similar process) to disclose all or any part of the Evaluation Materials, we or our representatives, as the case may be, agree to (i) immediately notify the Company of the existence, terms and circumstances surrounding such a request, (ii) consult with the Company on the advisability of taking legally available steps to resist or narrow such request and (iii) assist the Company in seeking a protective order or other appropriate remedy.
perturbation.changed_text: In the event that we or any of our representatives receive a request or are required (by deposition, interrogatory, request for documents, subpoena, civil investigative demand or similar process) to disclose all or any part of the Evaluation Materials, we or our representatives, as the case may be, agree to (i) notify the Company of the existence of such a request only if it is convenient, (ii) consult with the Company on the advisability of taking legally available steps to resist or narrow such request only if we deem it necessary, and (iii) assist the Company in seeking a protective order or other appropriate remedy at our sole discretion.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause mandates immediate notification and consultation with the company regarding requests to disclose Evaluation Materials. The modified version adds qualifiers like "only if it is convenient," "only if we deem it necessary," and "at our sole discretion," thereby contradicting the original intent of mandatory compliance and creating ambiguity. This now contradicts the earlier part of the section that stated we agree to certain actions when the request is received.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In the event that we or any of our representatives receive a request or are required (by deposition, interrogatory, request for documents, subpoena, civil investigative demand or similar process) to disclose all or any part of the Evaluation Materials, we or our representatives, as the case may be, agree to (i) notify the Company of the existence of such a request only if it is convenient, (ii) consult with the Company on the advisability of taking legally available steps to resist or narrow such request only if we deem it necessary, and (iii) assist the Company in seeking a protective order or other appropriate remedy at our sole discretion. In the event that such protective order or other remedy is not obtained or that the Company waives compliance with the provisions hereof, (i) we or our representatives, as the case may be, may disclose to any tribunal only that portion of the Evaluation Materials which we are advised by counsel is legally required to be disclosed, and shall exercise our best efforts to obtain assurance that confidential treatment will be accorded such Evaluation Materials and (ii) we shall not be liable for such disclosure unless disclosure to any such tribunal was caused by or resulted from a previous disclosure by us or our representatives not permitted by this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text mandates immediate notification and consultation. The changed text introduces qualifiers like 'only if it is convenient,' 'only if we deem it necessary,' and 'at our sole discretion.' These directly contradict the initial obligation to 'agree to' specific actions, creating a strong contradiction because the obligation has been changed to a conditional one.

perturbation.original_text: Accordingly, unless required by applicable law, we agree that prior to the execution of a Sale Agreement with respect to the closing of a Possible Transaction, without the prior written consent of the Company, we will not, and we will direct our representatives not to, disclose to any person either the fact that discussions or negotiations are taking place concerning a possible transaction between us and the Company or any to the terms, conditions or other facts with respect to any such Possible Transaction, including the status thereof unless we are advised by counsel that we are required to make such disclosure.
perturbation.changed_text: Accordingly, unless required by applicable law or if we feel it is in our best interest, we agree that prior to the execution of a Sale Agreement with respect to the closing of a Possible Transaction, without the prior written consent of the Company, we will not, and we will not necessarily direct our representatives not to, disclose to any person either the fact that discussions or negotiations are taking place concerning a possible transaction between us and the Company or any to the terms, conditions or other facts with respect to any such Possible Transaction, including the status thereof unless we are advised by counsel that we are required to make such disclosure.
perturbation.explanation: By adding 'or if we feel it is in our best interest' and changing 'we will direct our representatives not to' to 'we will not necessarily direct our representatives not to,' the clause introduces a subjective element and weakens the non-disclosure obligation. This now contradicts the earlier part of the section, creating in-text contradiction, implying a mandatory directive to maintain confidentiality.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Non-Disclosure The disclosure of our possible interest in purchasing the Company could have a material adverse effect on the Company's business if for any reason an agreement of purchase and sale is not consummated. Accordingly, unless required by applicable law or if we feel it is in our best interest, we agree that prior to the execution of a Sale Agreement with respect to the closing of a Possible Transaction, without the prior written consent of the Company, we will not, and we will not necessarily direct our representatives not to, disclose to any person either the fact that discussions or negotiations are taking place concerning a possible transaction between us and the Company or any to the terms, conditions or other facts with respect to any such Possible Transaction, including the status thereof unless we are advised by counsel that we are required to make such disclosure. The term "person" as used in this letter shall be broadly interpreted to include, without limitation, any corporation, the Company, governmental agency or body, stock exchange, partnership, association or individual.
perturbation.justification: The original text mandates non-disclosure and directs representatives to adhere to this. The changed text introduces the phrase 'or if we feel it is in our best interest' and weakens the directive regarding representatives ('we will not necessarily direct our representatives not to'). This creates a direct contradiction with the implied need to maintain confidentiality due to potential adverse effects on the Company's business. The change introduces a subjective element ('best interest') that can justify disclosure, thereby contradicting the original intent of strict non-disclosure. The Contradicted Text also contains the new phrase added, strengthening the idea that there is a contradiction as the statement as a whole now allows for potential disclosure, going against the first sentence of the Contradicted Text.
